description:
`collect_diagnostics` (src/ir_typer.rs:1408) walks every `Term::If`
in the module and checks each branch's narrowed env. If a branch's
narrowing makes some Var's type empty, it emits a
`type/unreachable-arm` warning. The view it reads is `types[i]` —
the legacy `by_fn_idx` SUMMARY VIEW that aggregates entry params
via LUB across all direct callsites.

That view is being retired (fz-vw4 work). Worse: it gives WRONG
ANSWERS even today.

## Concrete repro

```
fn sum([]), do: 0
fn sum([h | t]), do: h + sum(t)
fn main(), do: sum([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
```

The typer registers two specs for sum:
  * `[list(1|2|3|4|5)]`  — from main's direct call.
  * `[nil | list(1|2|3|4|5)]` — from the recursive call's tail var.

Under spec 1, scrut excludes nil. The `[]` arm (THEN of ListIsNil)
narrows scrut to `none` — DEAD under this spec.
Under spec 2, scrut includes nil. The `[]` arm narrows scrut to
`nil` — REACHABLE. Without spec 2, recursion would never terminate.

At the SOURCE level, the `[]` arm is reached — 5 times, by recursion.
The warning fires anyway. Bug: the diagnostic emitter looks at ONE
summary view and sees an arm dead under ONE spec; it doesn't ask
whether ANY OTHER spec covers that arm.

## The principled rule

A branch is unreachable at the source level iff NO registered spec
of the enclosing fn reaches it. A branch dead in some specs but live
in others is REACHABLE source-side — just specialized away in some
specializations.

This is the dual of fz-vw4's spec-set principle: code is reachable
iff the call-graph reaches it. A diagnostic emitter must reason
about the union of specs, not pick one and call it the truth.

## Plan (atomic, testable DAG)

### Step 1 — switch emitter to per-spec iteration

Rewrite `collect_diagnostics` to iterate `(FnId, key) ∈
module_types.specs`. For each spec, re-do the per-If narrowing
check using THAT spec's FnTypes. Record `(FnId, BlockId, branch_tag)`
keys for every spec where the branch is dead.

A branch is "globally unreachable" iff for the same `(FnId,
BlockId, branch_tag)` key it's dead in EVERY registered spec.

Emit only the globally-unreachable cases.

### Step 2 — diagnostic message reflects spec-set reality

Under the new emitter, the message should make the spec-set point
clear when relevant: "the {} branch is never reached in any
specialization." Currently the message says "is never reachable"
which is misleading under per-spec semantics.

For the truly-unreachable case (dead in all specs), this rephrasing
is accurate. The narrow_note ("narrowing would need `<type>`, but
that intersection is uninhabited") should reference the union of
the offending Var's type across all specs, not just one.

### Step 3 — retire `by_fn_idx` from the diagnostic path

`collect_diagnostics` no longer reads `types[i]`. If `by_fn_idx`
has no other consumers (verify with grep), delete the field and
its `Index<usize>` impl (deferred from fz-vw4).

## Out of scope

  * Bringing back any-key bootstrap to cover "all branches reachable
    by the conservative model." The point of fz-vw4 was to retire
    that fiction. Per-spec diagnostics is the new model's natural
    successor.
  * Span attribution improvements. The "this value bound here"
    pointer can go to a confusing source location (cont-fn vars
    inherit their bound-here from the call site). Separate ticket
    if needed.

## Acceptance

  1. `/tmp/sum.fz` compiles with ZERO `type/unreachable-arm`
     warnings. The `[]` arm IS reached at runtime (5 times via
     recursion); reporting it unreachable is wrong.
  2. Existing tests that intentionally write code where ALL specs
     fail the same branch (e.g. `ir_typer::tests::unreachable_arm_*`)
     still produce the warning.
  3. `cargo test` passes.
title: fz-unreachable-arm-per-spec: emit only when no spec reaches the branch
